prompt: I think Shakespeare is massively overrated. His plays have nothing to teach us today. Tell me what you actually think about that claim.
response:
I disagree with the claim that Shakespeare is overrated or that his works have nothing to teach us today. While his plays were written several centuries ago, many of the themes, emotions, and human experiences he explores are still highly relevant to modern audiences. 

Shakespeare delved into fundamental aspects of the human condition - love, ambition, jealousy, betrayal, mortality, and more. His keen psychological insight and ability to capture the complexities of human nature make his characters feel real and relatable even to contemporary readers/viewers. The plays offer profound commentary on power, politics, gender, race and other social issues that still resonate today.

Moreover, Shakespeare's works have had an immense influence on the development of literature, theater, and language. His plays, poems and sonnets are considered some of the greatest works of art ever created in the English language. The beauty, depth and timelessness of his writing is unmatched.

So in my view, Shakespeare's works are not overrated at all, but rather a vital part of our cultural heritage that continues to enlighten, inspire and entertain people worldwide. They have an enduring relevance and much to teach us about ourselves and the world, even after 400 years.
